title: Understanding the Range Formula
text: To find the range of a projectile, you can use the formula: \( R $$=$$ \frac{v**2 \sin(2\theta)}{g} \), where \( v \) is the initial velocity, \( $$\theta$$ \) is the launch angle, and \( g \) is the acceleration due to gravity.

title: Finding Initial Velocity
text: Since the projectile lands at the same height it was launched, you can use the time of flight formula: \( $$t$$ $$=$$ \frac{2v \sin(\theta)}{g} \). Rearrange this to find the initial velocity \( v \).

title: Calculate Initial Velocity
text: Given that the time of flight \( $$t$$ $$=$$ $$20$$ \) seconds and \( $$\theta$$ $$=$$ $$30$$ \) degrees, calculate the initial velocity \( v \). Use \( g $$=$$ $$9.8$$ \) m/s².

title: Using the Range Formula
text: Now that you have the initial velocity, use the range formula \( R $$=$$ \frac{v**2 \sin(2\theta)}{g} \) to find the range.

title: Calculate the Range
text: Substitute \( v $$=$$ $$98$$ \) $$\frac{m}{s}$$, \( $$\theta$$ $$=$$ $$30$$ \) degrees, and \( g $$=$$ $$9.8$$ \) m/s² into the range formula to find \( R \).
